1.definition: common title for a collection of resources. NOTE: Title identifies elements of a series collectively, combined with information about what volumes are available at the source cite.

2.definition: reference from one resource to another - TEST

3.definition: associated through a common heritage such as produced to a common product specification

4.definition: inverse of wasGeneratedBy where the domain resource generated the range resource implying that the domain resource is a temporal event, such as a data processing action, and the range resource is an entity

5.definition: inverse of wasDerivedFrom where the range resource was derived from the domain resource

7.definition: reference to resources that are parts of this resource

8.definition: reference to a master resource of which this one is a part

9.definition: inverse of operatesOn where the domain resource is, or can be, operated on (i.e.queried) by the range resource which must be an agent (i.e. is something that bears some form of responsibility for the operation taking place)

10.definition: an equivalent relationship to srv:operatesOn from a Service to a Dataset

11.definition: part of same structured set of data held in a computer

12.definition: resource is a revision of associated resource

13.definition: associated through a common heritage such as produced to a common product specification

14.definition: part of a set of imagery that when used together, provides three-dimensional images

15.definition: the domain resource was derived from the range resource which must be an entity (i.e. not an actor with agency or a temporal event)

16.definition: the domain resource was generated by the range resource which must have been a temporal event such as a data processing action or a field survey

17.definition: The domain resource was informed by the range resource which must have been an Entity (i.e. not an Agent with agency or an Activity temporal event). This is a looser association than wasDerivedFrom and does not indicate direct derivation, as wasDerivedFrom does.

18.definition: Location estimated from a printed map at 1:1 000 000 scale TEST

19.definition: Location estimated from a printed map at 1:100 000 scale

20.definition: Location estimated from a printed map at 1:25 000 scale

21.definition: Location estimated from an orthophoto at 1:25 000 scale

22.definition: Location estimated from a printed map at 1:250 000 scale

23.definition: Location estimated from a printed map at 1:50 000 scale

24.definition: Location estimated from a printed map at 1:500 000 scale

25.definition: Location estimated from an aerial photograph that has not been rectified to a known geographical datum or projection

26.definition: Location is estimated from an astronomical observation

27.prefLabel: astronomical observation and dead reckoning between fixes
27.definition: Location is estimated from an astronomical observation and dead reckoning between fixes.

28.definition: The location is supplied by a private company. The location method is not known.

29.definition: Location estimated from a dead reckoning observation

30.prefLabel: dead reckoning tied to transit satellite fixes
30.definition: Location estimated from a dead reckoning observation tied to transit satellite fixes

31.prefLabel: dead reckoning tied to transit satellite fixes and differential GPS
31.definition: Location estimated from a dead reckoning observation tied to transit satellite fixes and differential GPS

32.prefLabel: dead reckoning tied to transit satellite fixes and stand-alone GPS
32.definition: Location estimated from a dead reckoning observation tied to transit satellite fixes and stand alone GPS

33.definition: Location estimated from a digital elevation model or image

34.definition: Location estimated from a 9 second resolution digital elevation model or image

35.definition: Location estimated from a 90 metre resolution digital elevation model or image of data from the Shuttle Radar Topography Mission

36.definition: Location estimated from averaging a number of Global Positioning System observations

37.definition: Location estimated using DGPS, an enhancement to Global Positioning System which uses a network of fixed, ground-based reference stations to broadcast the difference between the positions indicated by the satellite systems and the known fixed positions.

38.definition: Location determined using DGPS, an enhancement to Global Positioning System which uses a network of fixed, ground-based reference stations to broadcast the difference between the positions indicated by the satellite systems and the known fixed positions.

39.definition: Location estimated from a Global Positioning System satellite navigation system

40.prefLabel: GPS observation subject to union all selective availability
40.definition: Location estimated from a Global Positioning System prior to May 2000 before when the US government commonly degraded the accuracy of GPS signals by adding intentional, time varying errors of up to 100 meters to the publicly available navigation signal.

41.definition: Location estimated from GPS observation with P-Code access

42.definition: Location estimated from a printed topographic, geological, or other map

43.definition: Location estimated from an aerial photograph geometrically corrected ("orthorectified") such that the scale across the image is uniform

44.definition: The location method is known, but is not specified in this vocabulary

45.definition: The location is supplied via personal communication.  The location method is not known.

46.definition: The location is taken from a published report or article.  The location method is not known.

48.prefLabel: satellite Doppler and AVLF radio navigation
48.definition: Location estimated from satellite Doppler and AVLF radio navigation

49.definition: Location estimated from a satellite image

50.definition: Location estimated from a satellite image provided by Google Earth or Google Maps

51.prefLabel: State or NT agency supplied location, method unknown
51.definition: The location is supplied by a State or Territory government agency. The location method is not known.

52.definition: Location estimated using survey from ground control

53.definition: The location method is unknown. However, the correct value probably exists.

54.definition: The location is taken from an unpublished report.  The location method is not known.
